title: Man page generation research
content:
## How cmdliner generates man pages

Cmdliner generates man pages at **runtime**, not build time. Every cmdliner-based tool automatically supports `--help=groff`, which outputs the tool's help in groff man page format. The man page content is derived from:

- `Cmd.info` (name, synopsis, description)
- Argument `doc` strings (positional and optional)
- `Cmd.Exit.info` and `Cmd.Env.info` entries
- Manual `man` blocks passed to `Cmd.info`

The `Cmdliner.Manpage.print` function can also be used programmatically with format `Groff`.

## How other tools ship man pages

Dune, cmdliner itself, and cmarkit all install man pages into `~/.opam/default/man/man1/`. Cmdliner 2.x ships a dedicated `cmdliner install tool-manpages` command that runs the tool executable, extracts groff output for each subcommand, and writes man pages to a target directory. It can also patch a `.install` file for opam integration.

## Effort to enable man page installation

For dune >= 3.5 with directory-targets, it is straightforward. Add to bin/dune:

```
(rule
 (target (dir cmdliner-support))
 (deps main.exe)
 (action
  (ignore-stdout
   (run cmdliner install tool-support ./main.exe:bs cmdliner-support))))

(install
 (section share_root)
 (dirs (cmdliner-support/share as .)))
```

This generates man pages for bs and all subcommands during `dune build @install`. Alternatively, the simpler opam-only approach uses an opam.template file with a `cmdliner install tool-support --update-opam-install` post-build step.

## Recommendation

For an initial release, `--help` (and `--help=groff` for power users) is sufficient. Man page installation adds a small amount of build complexity and a hard dependency on cmdliner's install tool being available. It is a nice-to-have for a polished release but not blocking.

title: Plan: feature gaps for agent-oriented knowledge bases
content:
## Required reading

- `docs/index.md`, `docs/lib/architecture.md`, `docs/lib/correct-construction.md`
- `docs/lib/principles.md`, `docs/bin/principles.md`, `docs/test/principles.md`
- `docs/test-integration/architecture.md`, `docs/test-integration/principles.md`
- `docs/product-requirements.md`
- `docs/designs/design-feature-gaps.md` — the design being implemented

## Goal

Implement 9 features from the feature gaps design: timestamps, repo delete ops, reopen, delete, unrelate, graph traversal, list query expansion, statistics, and GC. These close gaps that limit `bs` as a primary tool for coding agents.

## Current state

**Data types** — `Todo.t`/`Note.t`: `id`, `niceid`, `title`, `content`, `status`. No timestamps. `make` takes 5 positional args.

**Repository** — `Relation` has no per-relation `delete` or `delete_by_entity`. `Niceid` has no per-entry `delete`. JSONL `entity_record` has no timestamp fields.

**Service** — No `reopen`, `delete`, `unrelate`, `Graph_service`, or `Gc_service`. `Query_service.list` takes individual params, no sort/count/relation-filter support.

**CLI** — 13 commands. No reopen/delete/unrelate/gc. `list` has `--status`, `--available`, `--json` only. `show` has no timestamp lines.

## Target state

**Data types** — `Todo.t`/`Note.t` gain `created_at:int`, `updated_at:int`. New `Data.Timestamp` module. `make` gains `~created_at ~updated_at`.

**Repository** — `Relation` gains `delete`, `delete_by_entity`. `Niceid` gains `delete`. JSONL `entity_record` gains timestamps. Schema adds timestamp columns.

**Service** — `Mutation_service` gains `reopen`, sets `updated_at`. New `Delete_service` (cascade + blocking guard). `Relation_service` gains `unrelate`. New `Graph_service` (BFS). `Query_service.list` takes `list_spec` record. New `Gc_service`. GC hooks into `open_kb`.

**CLI** — 4 new commands: reopen, delete, unrelate, gc. `list` gains `--sort`, `--asc`, `--count`, relation filters, `--transitive`. `show` gains timestamp lines. 17 commands total.

## Design decisions

1. **Timestamps in data types, not a wrapper.** More invasive but cleaner — types fully describe the entity, no indirection.
2. **`updated_at` is a service concern.** `with_*` updaters don't touch timestamps. Service sets it after no-op detection. Keeps data layer pure.
3. **Application-level BFS.** Matches `find_blockers` pattern. Testable, sufficient for scale. API is implementation-agnostic.
4. **Query spec record.** Replaces growing param list with `list_spec` record. Idiomatic, self-contained validation.
5. **Service-layer delete cascade.** Not SQL cascade. Matches thin-repo + service-logic pattern.
6. **GC hooks into `open_kb`.** Every command sees clean state. One change site. Consistent with `rebuild_if_needed`.

## Atomic groups

**Group A: Timestamps** (tasks 1.1–1.4) — Changing `make` signatures breaks compilation everywhere. Data type, repo, JSONL, and service changes are inseparable.

**Group B: Delete cascade** (tasks 4.1–4.3) — Service requires repo ops from step 2. CLI is the only consumer. Land together.

**Group C: List query expansion** (tasks 7.1–7.3) — Changing `list` from params to record breaks the Kb_service → Query_service → Cmd_list chain.

title: Plan: feature gaps — impact analysis
content:
## Impact analysis

### Data layer (lib/data/)
- `todo.mli`/`.ml` — add `created_at`, `updated_at` fields, accessors, `with_updated_at`
- `note.mli`/`.ml` — same as todo
- `timestamp.mli`/`.ml` — **new** — ISO 8601 formatting/parsing

### Repository layer (lib/repository/)
- `todo.mli`/`.ml` — schema gains columns; `create`/`import` gain timestamps
- `note.mli`/`.ml` — same as todo repo
- `relation.mli`/`.ml` — add `delete`, `delete_by_entity`, `Not_found` error variant
- `niceid.mli`/`.ml` — add per-entry `delete` (returns `Not_found` if absent)
- `jsonl.mli`/`.ml` — `entity_record` gains timestamps; serializer/parser updated

### Service layer (lib/service/)
- `mutation_service.mli`/`.ml` — add `reopen`; set `updated_at` in update path
- `query_service.mli`/`.ml` — `list_spec` record; sort/count/relation-filter logic
- `relation_service.mli`/`.ml` — add `unrelate`
- `kb_service.mli`/`.ml` — add reopen/delete/unrelate; update `list` sig; GC hook in `open_kb`; `_run_gc` flushes via `Sync_service` directly (not `_with_flush`)
- `sync_service.ml` — pass timestamps through rebuild
- `delete_service.mli`/`.ml` — **new** — cascade delete with blocking guard
- `graph_service.mli`/`.ml` — **new** — BFS reachability, connected components
- `gc_service.mli`/`.ml` — **new** — collect eligible, run cleanup

### CLI layer (bin/)
- `main.ml` — register 4 new commands
- `cmd_init.ml` — write default `gc_max_age` (30d) to config, output confirmation line
- `cmd_show.ml` — add timestamp lines (text + JSON)
- `cmd_list.ml` — add --sort, --asc, --count, relation filters, --transitive
- `cmd_reopen.ml` — **new** (~45 lines)
- `cmd_delete.ml` — **new** (~65 lines)
- `cmd_unrelate.ml` — **new** (~55 lines)
- `cmd_gc.ml` — **new** (~80 lines)
- `dune` — add new modules

### Tests
- `test/data/todo_expect.ml`, `note_expect.ml` — all `make` calls gain timestamps
- `test/data/timestamp_expect.ml` — **new**
- `test/repository/*_expect.ml` — snapshot updates, new delete tests
- `test/service/*_expect.ml` — reopen, sort, count, filter, unrelate tests
- `test/service/delete_service_expect.ml` — **new**
- `test/service/graph_service_expect.ml` — **new**
- `test/service/gc_service_expect.ml` — **new**
- Integration tests: new files for reopen, delete, unrelate, gc, extended list
- Integration tests: `workflow_expect.ml` gains scenarios exercising new commands
- `test-perf/perf_scenarios.ml` — add `scenario_gc`

### Files that do NOT change
- `lib/data/relation*`, `identifier*`, `lib/control/*` — unchanged
- `lib/repository/config.ml`, `sqlite.ml`, `root.ml` — unchanged
- `bin/cmd_add.ml`, `cmd_update.ml`, `cmd_resolve.ml`, `cmd_archive.ml` — unchanged
- `bin/cmd_claim.ml`, `cmd_next.ml`, `cmd_flush.ml`, `cmd_rebuild.ml` — unchanged
- `bin/app_context.ml` — unchanged

title: Code review: feature-gaps implementation
content:
Reviewed the unstaged changes implementing design-feature-gaps.md.

Accepted suggestions:
1. TypeidSet duplicated in 3 service modules -> extract to Data.Uuid.Typeid.Set
2. Item accessors duplicated across services -> create Data.Item with shared type and accessors
3. _map_niceid_err duplicated -> add map_niceid_repo_error to Item_service
4. _parse_gc_age duplicated in kb_service and cmd_gc -> move to Gc_service as public
6. of_iso8601 UTC offset fragile -> replace with module-level constant, add comment
7. cmd_gc.ml deeply nested -> extract helper functions
8. cmd_gc.ml inits App_context 3 times -> lift to top of run
10. GC default age duplicated -> share constant via Gc_service

Rejected:
5. JSON helpers in jsonl.ml vs test_helper.ml - different purposes, leave separate
9. Design doc - already consistent, no work needed

title: Code review: bin/ directory
content:
Review of bin/ directory changes covering new commands (delete, gc, reopen, unrelate), expanded list/show, and init gc_max_age.

Accepted:
1. gc_max_age accepted but never persisted in init_kb — implement persistence + integration test
2. Warning 32 suppression added in this diff — remove it, _ prefix suffices
3. cmd_list duplicates build_specs relation filter logic — use build_specs
4. cmd_gc leaks resolution logic into bin/ — move get/set to service, keep display in bin
5. app_context.root exposes repository handle — remove once #4 is done
6. cmd_show and cmd_init missing --json examples — add them
7. _blocking silently ignored in cmd_list and cmd_unrelate — implement in list, clean up unrelate

Rejected: none

title: Code review: lib/ directory
content: Reviewed lib/ directory changes. Accepted all 6 suggestions: (1) extract Timestamp.now to eliminate _now() duplication, (2) extract map_config_error into Item_service, (3) add created_at/updated_at to Data.Item, (4) split show_service from query_service to stay under 300 lines, (5) extract cascade-delete helper in delete_service for gc_service reuse, (6) make Data.Timestamp.t an abstract type per Correct Construction.

title: Code review: test changes
content: Review of test changes on main branch. Accepted: (1) split query_service_expect.ml >300 lines, (2) split relation_service_expect.ml >300 lines, (3) eliminate Unix.sleepf in unit tests via time injection + add testing principle, (4) restore bs list note --available validation error, (5) fix <missing> sentinel test in list_json_expect.ml, (6) verify dependent item in delete --force test, (7) add delete/gc/reopen/unrelate to workflow tests. Rejected: none.

title: Plan: uninstall command and init action refactor
content:
## Required reading

- `docs/lib/architecture.md` — layering rules, where new code belongs
- `docs/lib/principles.md` — correct construction, file length, nesting
- `docs/bin/principles.md` — CLI conventions, `--json` requirement, help text, integration test mandate
- `docs/test/principles.md` — state assertions, temp file cleanup, file length
- `docs/test-integration/principles.md` — cleanup, file length
- `docs/test-integration/architecture.md` — naming, helpers, coverage expectations

## Goal

Implement a `bs uninstall` command that cleanly reverses the effects of `bs init`. The init side effects (database creation, config persistence, AGENTS.md installation, git exclude entry) are currently entangled in `Lifecycle.init_kb` with no reverse path. The change refactors init's side effects into discrete helpers and adds reverse counterparts, then builds a new `uninstall` CLI command on top. Init's observable behavior must not change.

## Current state

`Lifecycle.init_kb` performs five sequential steps inline:

```ocaml
(* lifecycle.ml — current structure *)
let init_kb ~directory ~namespace ~gc_max_age =
  let* directory = resolve_directory directory in
  let* namespace = resolve_namespace ~directory namespace in
  let db_file = Filename.concat directory db_filename in
  if Sys.file_exists db_file then Error (Validation_error ...)
  else
    (* Database creation — inline *)
    let root = Root.init ~db_file ~namespace in
    Fun.protect ~finally:(fun () -> Root.close root) (fun () ->
      Config.set ... ;  Config.set ... ;
      let agents_md = install_agents_md ~directory in
      let git_exclude = install_git_exclude ~directory in
      Ok { directory; namespace; db_file; agents_md; git_exclude })
```

Helpers `install_agents_md` and `install_git_exclude` exist as standalone functions. Database creation is inline. There are no reverse helpers and no `uninstall_kb` function. `Kb_service` re-exports init types verbatim.

Types:
```ocaml
type agents_md_action = Created | Appended | Already_present
type git_exclude_action = Excluded | Already_excluded
type init_result = { directory; namespace; db_file; agents_md; git_exclude }
```

No `delete_file` in `Control.Io`. `Sys.remove` used only in test helpers.

## Target state

`lifecycle.ml` gains three uninstall helpers alongside the existing install helpers, plus an `uninstall_kb` orchestrator:

```ocaml
(* New types *)
type file_action = Deleted | Not_found
type agents_md_uninstall_action =
  | File_deleted | Section_removed | Section_modified | Not_found
type git_exclude_uninstall_action = Entry_removed | Entry_not_found

type uninstall_result = {
  directory   : string;
  database    : file_action;
  jsonl       : file_action;
  agents_md   : agents_md_uninstall_action;
  git_exclude : git_exclude_uninstall_action;
}

(* New helpers *)
val uninstall_file        : string -> file_action
val uninstall_agents_md   : directory:string -> agents_md_uninstall_action
val uninstall_git_exclude : directory:string -> git_exclude_uninstall_action
val uninstall_kb          : directory:string option -> (uninstall_result, error) result
```

Database creation is extracted to `install_database` (internal helper, not exposed). `init_kb` calls it instead of inline code — same behavior.

`Kb_service` re-exports the new types and `uninstall_kb`.

`bin/cmd_uninstall.ml` is a new CLI command gated by `--yes`, supporting `-d` and `--json`. Registered in `main.ml`.

## Impact analysis

**lib/service/lifecycle.ml** — Extract `install_database`, add 3 uninstall helpers + `uninstall_kb` orchestrator + new types. ~80 lines added.
**lib/service/lifecycle.mli** — Export new types and `uninstall_kb`. ~20 lines added.
**lib/service/kb_service.ml** — Re-export uninstall types, add `uninstall_kb` wrapper. ~15 lines added.
**lib/service/kb_service.mli** — Re-export uninstall types and signature. ~15 lines added.
**bin/cmd_uninstall.ml** — New file. CLI argument parsing, `--yes` gate, output formatting (text + JSON). ~80 lines.
**bin/main.ml** — Add `Cmd_uninstall.cmd` to subcommand group + help text. ~3 lines changed.
**test/lifecycle_expect.ml** — New unit tests for each uninstall helper and roundtrip. ~100 lines added.
**test-integration/uninstall_expect.ml** — New file. Integration tests for all 8 scenarios. ~200 lines.

**Files that do NOT change** (worth confirming):
- `lib/service/git.ml` — uninstall reads `.git/info/exclude` directly via `Io.read_file`, no new Git module functions needed.
- `lib/control/io.ml` — uses `Sys.remove` directly for deletion, no new Io helper.
- `bin/cmd_init.ml` — init behavior unchanged.
- `test/lifecycle_expect.ml` existing tests — must pass without modification.
- `test-integration/init_expect.ml` — must pass without modification.

## Design decisions

1. **`Sys.remove` directly, no `Io.delete_file` wrapper.** The design doc notes that no file deletion exists in production code. `uninstall_file` uses `Sys.remove` directly rather than adding to `Io`. Deletion is a one-off need specific to uninstall, not a general utility. If more callers emerge, extraction is trivial.

2. **`install_database` is internal.** Extracted from `init_kb` for clarity but not exposed in `.mli`. It is not independently testable via the public interface — the existing `init_kb` tests cover it. This avoids expanding the module's public API for a refactoring convenience.

3. **`--yes` checked in CLI layer.** Following the `--force` pattern in `cmd_delete.ml`, the confirmation gate is in `cmd_uninstall.ml`. The service function `uninstall_kb` always proceeds. This keeps the safety check where it belongs (user-facing layer).

4. **Uninstall uses `resolve_directory` directly, not the full init precondition chain.** It does not validate namespace or check database existence — partial teardown is supported by the best-effort property.

5. **`Section_modified` warns without removing.** When the AGENTS.md heading is present but the exact template does not match, uninstall reports the discrepancy rather than attempting heuristic removal.

6. **`String.contains_substring_at` not needed.** The design uses `String.sub` for index-based extraction in `uninstall_agents_md`. This is implemented directly using stdlib `String` functions rather than adding a new `Data.String` helper.

## Atomic groups

**Group A: Types + helpers + orchestration** (tasks 2–5)
Tasks 2–5 add new types and functions to `lifecycle.ml`/`.mli`. Each task adds types needed by subsequent tasks. They can be committed individually (each leaves the codebase compiling), but they form a logical unit — the uninstall service layer.

**Group B: CLI + integration tests** (task 6)
`cmd_uninstall.ml`, `main.ml` registration, and `uninstall_expect.ml` must land together — the integration tests invoke the binary, which requires the command to exist.

title: Code review: bin/ uninstall command
content:
Reviewed bin/ changes for the uninstall command.

Accepted suggestions:
1. Make --yes flag ~doc more descriptive about why it exists (destructive operation), but keep it concise.
2. Regroup commands in main.ml help text so related commands are adjacent (lifecycle, create, query, workflow, relations, maintenance). Add a bin principle about this.
3. Add an uninstall example to the root help text EXAMPLES section.

No suggestions rejected.

title: Code review: uninstall test coverage
content: Reviewed test/ and test-integration/ for the uninstall feature. Two suggestions accepted: (1) Add direct unit tests for Git.add_exclude and Git.remove_exclude in git_expect.ml — currently only tested indirectly through lifecycle wrappers. (2) Add a --json error-path integration test for uninstall.

title: 1.1 Add timestamp fields to Data.Todo and Data.Note
content:
## Files
- `lib/data/todo.mli` / `todo.ml`
- `lib/data/note.mli` / `note.ml`
- `test/data/todo_expect.ml`
- `test/data/note_expect.ml`

## Description
- Add `created_at : int` and `updated_at : int` fields to both record types.
- Extend `make` signature: `... -> created_at:int -> updated_at:int -> t`.
- Add accessors: `val created_at : t -> int`, `val updated_at : t -> int`.
- Add updater: `val with_updated_at : t -> int -> t`.
- `pp`/`show` (ppx_deriving) will include timestamp fields automatically.
- Update all `make` calls in data-layer tests to pass `~created_at:0 ~updated_at:0`.

## TDD note
- Write tests first that construct todos/notes with timestamp args and assert accessors return expected values.
- Write test for `with_updated_at` returning a copy with the new value.
- Tests won't compile until `make` signature changes — that's RED.
- Implement the type changes — that's GREEN.

title: 1.2 Create Data.Timestamp module
content:
## Files
- `lib/data/timestamp.mli` / `timestamp.ml` — **new**
- `test/data/timestamp_expect.ml` — **new**

## Description
- New module providing ISO 8601 formatting and parsing at the JSONL boundary.
- `val to_iso8601 : int -> string` — converts epoch seconds to `"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ"`.
- `val of_iso8601 : string -> (int, string) result` — parses ISO 8601 to epoch seconds.
- `val to_display : int -> string` — formats as `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC"` for text output.
- Built on `Unix.gmtime` / `Unix.mktime` and `Printf.sprintf` / `Scanf.sscanf`. No new deps.

## TDD note
- Write round-trip tests: `of_iso8601 (to_iso8601 epoch) = Ok epoch` for several values.
- Write `to_display` formatting tests.
- Write error cases: malformed strings, empty string.
- Tests won't compile until module exists — RED. Implement — GREEN.

title: 1.3 Add timestamps to repository layer and JSONL
content:
## Files
- `lib/repository/todo.mli` / `todo.ml`
- `lib/repository/note.mli` / `note.ml`
- `lib/repository/jsonl.mli` / `jsonl.ml`
- `test/repository/todo_repo_expect.ml`
- `test/repository/note_repo_expect.ml`
- `test/repository/jsonl_expect.ml`

## Description
- SQLite schema: add `created_at INTEGER NOT NULL`, `updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL` to both tables.
- `create` functions: generate `now` internally for both timestamps.
- `import` functions: accept `~created_at ~updated_at` params from JSONL data.
- SQL INSERT/SELECT statements updated to include timestamp columns.
- JSONL `entity_record` gains `created_at: int; updated_at: int` fields.
- JSONL serializer: write `created_at`/`updated_at` as ISO 8601 strings.
- JSONL parser: read ISO 8601 strings, convert to int via `Timestamp.of_iso8601`.
- `record_sort_key` unchanged (sorts by TypeId).

## TDD note
- Update JSONL round-trip tests to include timestamp fields — expect failures until serializer/parser updated.
- Update repo tests to verify timestamps are persisted and retrieved correctly.
- Test that `import` preserves caller-provided timestamps.
- Test that `create` sets both timestamps to the same value (now).

title: 1.4 Set updated_at in service layer and update show/list output
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/mutation_service.ml`
- `lib/service/sync_service.ml`
- `bin/cmd_show.ml`
- `bin/cmd_list.ml`
- `test/service/mutation_service_expect.ml`
- `test/service/sync_service_expect.ml`

## Description
- `Mutation_service.update`: after no-op detection passes, call `with_updated_at` with `now` before persisting. The sequence: find → apply field changes → no-op check → set updated_at → repo.update.
- `Mutation_service.resolve`, `archive`: these delegate to `update` internally, so they get `updated_at` for free.
- `Sync_service.force_rebuild`: pass timestamps from JSONL `entity_record` to `import`. For old JSONL files without timestamps, assign `now` as synthetic value.
- `cmd_show.ml` text output: add `Created:` and `Updated:` lines after `Status:` using `Timestamp.to_display`.
- `cmd_show.ml` JSON output: add `created_at` and `updated_at` fields using `Timestamp.to_iso8601`.
- `cmd_list.ml` JSON output: add `created_at` and `updated_at` per item. Text output unchanged.

## TDD note
- Write mutation_service test: update an item, verify `updated_at` changes while `created_at` stays.
- Write test: no-op update does NOT change `updated_at`.
- Write sync_service test: rebuild from JSONL with timestamps preserves them.
- Update show integration test snapshots for new timestamp lines.

title: 2.1 Add delete to Repository.Relation
content:
## Files
- `lib/repository/relation.mli` / `relation.ml`
- `test/repository/relation_repo_expect.ml`

## Description
- Add `Not_found` variant to `error` type.
- `val delete : t -> source:Data.Uuid.Typeid.t -> target:Data.Uuid.Typeid.t -> kind:Data.Relation_kind.t -> bidirectional:bool -> (unit, error) result` — deletes a single relation by composite key. If `bidirectional` is true and no row found for (source, target, kind), tries (target, source, kind). Returns `Not_found` if neither exists.
- `val delete_by_entity : t -> Data.Uuid.Typeid.t -> (int, error) result` — deletes all relations where typeid appears as source or target. Returns count of deleted rows.

## TDD note
- Write test: create a relation, delete it, verify list_all is empty.
- Write test: delete bidirectional relation from either endpoint.
- Write test: delete non-existent relation returns `Not_found`.
- Write test: `delete_by_entity` removes all relations involving the entity and returns correct count.

title: 2.2 Add delete to Repository.Niceid
content:
## Files
- `lib/repository/niceid.mli` / `niceid.ml`
- `test/repository/niceid_expect.ml`

## Description
- Add `Not_found` variant to `error` type (matching `Repository.Relation`'s convention).
- `val delete : t -> Data.Uuid.Typeid.t -> (unit, error) result` — removes the niceid mapping for a given TypeId. Uses `DELETE FROM niceid WHERE typeid = ?`. Returns `Not_found` if no row was affected (consistent with `Repository.Relation.delete`).

## TDD note
- Write test: allocate a niceid, delete it, verify a new allocation doesn't conflict.
- Write test: delete non-existent typeid returns `Not_found`.

title: 3. Add reopen to Mutation_service and Cmd_reopen
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/mutation_service.mli` / `mutation_service.ml`
- `lib/service/kb_service.mli` / `kb_service.ml`
- `bin/cmd_reopen.mli` / `cmd_reopen.ml` — **new**
- `bin/main.ml`
- `bin/dune`
- `test/service/mutation_service_expect.ml`
- Integration tests for reopen

## Description
- `Mutation_service.reopen : t -> identifier:string -> (Item_service.item, Item_service.error) result` — resolves identifier, checks terminal state (Done for todos, Archived for notes), delegates to `update` with initial status (Open/Active). Returns `Validation_error "not in terminal state"` for non-terminal items.
- `Kb_service.reopen` — delegates to mutation_service, wrapped in `_with_flush`.
- `Cmd_reopen` — follows `cmd_resolve.ml` pattern (~45 lines). Text: "Reopened todo: kb-0" or "Reactivated note: kb-1". JSON: `{ok, action:"reopened", type, niceid}`.
- Register in `main.ml`, add to `(modules ...)` in `bin/dune`.
- Help text: EXAMPLES section covering reopen of a todo and a note, error case (non-terminal), and `--json` example.

## TDD note
- Write test: resolve a todo, reopen it, verify status is Open and `updated_at` changed.
- Write test: archive a note, reopen it, verify status is Active.
- Write test: reopen an Open todo returns Validation_error.
- Write test: reopen a non-existent item returns error.
- Integration tests: `bs reopen kb-0` after `bs resolve kb-0`.
- Integration test: auto-rebuild (delete `.kbases.db`, run `bs reopen`, assert success).
- Integration test: add a reopen scenario to `workflow_expect.ml` (e.g., create → resolve → reopen → resolve again).

title: 4. Create Delete_service and Cmd_delete
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/delete_service.mli` / `delete_service.ml` — **new**
- `lib/service/kb_service.mli` / `kb_service.ml`
- `bin/cmd_delete.mli` / `cmd_delete.ml` — **new**
- `bin/main.ml`
- `bin/dune`
- `test/service/delete_service_expect.ml` — **new**
- Integration tests for delete

## Description
- New `Delete_service` module with types: `delete_result = { niceid; entity_type; relations_removed }`, `delete_error = Blocked_dependency of { niceid; dependents } | Service_error of Item_service.error`.
- `val delete : t -> identifier:string -> force:bool -> (delete_result, delete_error) result` — cascade: resolve → blocking check (if not force) → delete_by_entity → delete niceid → delete item.
- `val delete_many : t -> identifiers:string list -> force:bool -> (delete_result list, delete_error) result` — processes each independently. If any fails blocking check, entire batch fails before any deletion.
- **Transaction ownership**: the transaction boundary lives in `Kb_service.delete`/`delete_many`, not in `Delete_service`, following the existing pattern where `kb_service.ml` wraps `Repository.Sqlite.with_transaction` around service calls (see `relate`, `add_*_with_relations`). `Delete_service` assumes it is called within a transaction.
- `Kb_service.delete` / `delete_many` — wraps in `_with_flush` + `with_transaction`.
- `Cmd_delete` — accepts multiple positional identifiers + `--force` flag (~65 lines). Text: "Deleted todo: kb-0" per item. JSON: `{ok, deleted: [{type, niceid, relations_removed}]}`.
- Help text: EXAMPLES section covering single delete, batch delete, `--force`, blocked error, and `--json` example.

## TDD note
- Write test: delete an item, verify it's gone from list and show returns not-found.
- Write test: delete item with relations, verify relations also removed.
- Write test: delete item that is blocking target of non-terminal source → Blocked_dependency error.
- Write test: delete with force=true ignores blocking check.
- Write test: batch delete, one blocked → all fail.
- Write test: delete a non-existent item returns error.
- Integration tests: `bs delete kb-0`, `bs delete kb-0 --force`, `bs delete kb-0 kb-1`.
- Integration test: auto-rebuild (delete `.kbases.db`, run `bs delete`, assert success).
- Integration test: add a delete scenario to `workflow_expect.ml`.

title: 5. Add unrelate to Relation_service and Cmd_unrelate
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/relation_service.mli` / `relation_service.ml`
- `lib/service/kb_service.mli` / `kb_service.ml`
- `bin/cmd_unrelate.mli` / `cmd_unrelate.ml` — **new**
- `bin/main.ml`
- `bin/dune`
- `test/service/relation_service_expect.ml`
- Integration tests for unrelate

## Description
- `Relation_service.unrelate : t -> source:string -> specs:relate_spec list -> (unrelate_result list, Item_service.error) result` — mirrors `relate_many`: resolve source and targets, validate kinds, call `Repository.Relation.delete` for each. `unrelate_result = { source_niceid; target_niceid; kind; bidirectional }`.
- `Kb_service.unrelate` — delegates, wrapped in `_with_flush`.
- `Cmd_unrelate` — reuses shared relation flags from `cmdline_common.ml` (~55 lines). Text: "Unrelated: kb-2 related-to kb-4 (removed)". JSON: `{ok, removed: [{source, kind, target, directionality}]}`.
- Help text: EXAMPLES section covering unrelate with each relation kind, bidirectional from either side, and `--json` example.

## TDD note
- Write test: create a relation, unrelate it, verify it's gone.
- Write test: unrelate bidirectional relation from either endpoint.
- Write test: unrelate non-existent relation returns error.
- Integration tests: `bs unrelate kb-0 --depends-on kb-1`, bidirectional from either side.
- Integration test: auto-rebuild (delete `.kbases.db`, run `bs unrelate`, assert success).
- Integration test: add an unrelate scenario to `workflow_expect.ml`.

title: 6. Create Graph_service module
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/graph_service.mli` / `graph_service.ml` — **new**
- `test/service/graph_service_expect.ml` — **new**

## Description
- New module providing reachability queries over the relation graph via BFS.
- `val init : Repository.Root.t -> t`
- `val reachable_from : t -> typeid:Data.Uuid.Typeid.t -> kind:Data.Relation_kind.t option -> direction:[Outgoing | Incoming | Any] -> (Data.Uuid.Typeid.t list, Item_service.error) result` — BFS from typeid following relations of given kind in given direction. Returns all reachable TypeIds.
- `val connected_component : t -> typeid:Data.Uuid.Typeid.t -> (Data.Uuid.Typeid.t list, Item_service.error) result` — all TypeIds reachable following all kinds in both directions. Used by GC.
- Implementation: application-level BFS using find_by_source/find_by_target. Visited set prevents cycles. Consistent with existing find_blockers pattern.

## TDD note
- Write test: linear chain A->B->C, reachable_from A Outgoing returns {B, C}.
- Write test: reachable_from C Incoming returns {A, B}.
- Write test: diamond graph, verify no duplicates.
- Write test: cycle A->B->C->A — BFS terminates and returns all three nodes exactly once (validates visited-set logic).
- Write test: kind filter — only follows matching relations.
- Write test: connected_component returns entire component regardless of direction.
- Write test: connected_component on a cycle returns exactly the cycle members.
- Write test: disconnected items not included.

title: 7. Expand list with sort, count, relation filters, and --transitive
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/query_service.mli` / `query_service.ml`
- `lib/service/kb_service.mli` / `kb_service.ml`
- `bin/cmd_list.ml`
- `test/service/query_service_expect.ml`
- Integration tests for extended list

## Description
- New types in `query_service.mli`: `sort_order = Sort_created | Sort_updated`, `relation_filter = { target; kind; direction }`, `list_spec = { entity_type; statuses; available; sort; ascending; count_only; relation_filters; transitive }`, `list_result = Items of item list | Counts of { todos; notes }`.
- Replace `list` signature: `val list : t -> list_spec -> (list_result, error) result`.
- Flag interaction validation upfront: `available + statuses → error`, `sort + count → error`, `transitive without filter → error`, `transitive with >1 filter → error`.
- Sort comparator: None → niceid order (ascending), Sort_created/Sort_updated → descending by default, `ascending` flag reverses.
- Relation filters: resolve targets, use `Graph_service.reachable_from` if transitive, direct `find_by_source`/`find_by_target` if not. Intersect with type/status-filtered items.
- Count: fetch items through normal path, group by type and status. Text format: "3 open todos, 1 in-progress todo" etc. JSON: `{ok, counts: {todos: {open: 3}, notes: {active: 4}}}`.
- `Kb_service.list` signature changes to take `list_spec`.
- `Cmd_list` gains: `--sort created|updated`, `--asc`, `--count`, `--depends-on`, `--related-to`, `--uni`, `--bi`, `--transitive` flags.
- Provide a `default_list_spec` value in `query_service.mli` with sensible defaults (all filters empty, ascending=true for niceid, count_only=false, transitive=false). This avoids 8-field record construction boilerplate in tests.

## TDD note
- Write test: list with Sort_created returns items in descending created_at order.
- Write test: list with ascending=true reverses order.
- Write test: count_only returns correct counts grouped by type/status.
- Write test: relation filter returns only items related to target.
- Write test: transitive filter follows chain.
- Write test: invalid flag combos return Validation_error.
- Integration tests for all new flag combinations.
- Help text: EXAMPLES section covering every new flag and common combinations, including `--json` example.

title: 8. Add --count statistics to list
content:
## Files
- Implemented as part of task 7 (list query expansion)
- `bin/cmd_list.ml` — count output formatting
- `test/service/query_service_expect.ml` — count-specific tests
- Integration tests for `bs list --count`

## Description
- This task is logically part of task 7 but called out separately per the design.
- When `count_only = true` in `list_spec`, fetch items through normal path, group by type and status.
- Text format: "3 open todos, 1 in-progress todo, 2 done todos\n4 active notes, 1 archived note".
- JSON format: `{"ok": true, "counts": {"todos": {"open": 3, "in-progress": 1, "done": 2}, "notes": {"active": 4, "archived": 1}}}`.
- Composes with type filter: `bs list todo --count` shows only todo counts.
- Composes with `--available`: `bs list --available --count` shows "N available todos".

## TDD note
- Write test: count with no filter returns all status counts.
- Write test: count with type filter returns only that type's counts.
- Write test: count with --available returns available count only.
- Write test: --sort + --count returns Validation_error.
- Write test: --count + relation filter (e.g., `bs list --depends-on kb-5 --count`) returns counts of filtered items only (requirement 19 composition).

title: 9. Create Gc_service and Cmd_gc
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/gc_service.mli` / `gc_service.ml` — **new**
- `lib/service/kb_service.ml` — GC hook in `open_kb`
- `bin/cmd_gc.mli` / `cmd_gc.ml` — **new**
- `bin/cmd_init.ml` — add `--gc-max-age` flag, write value (or 30d default) to config, output confirmation
- `bin/main.ml`
- `bin/dune`
- `test/service/gc_service_expect.ml` — **new**
- `test-perf/perf_scenarios.ml` — `scenario_gc`
- Integration tests for gc

## Description
- New `Gc_service` with types: `gc_result = { items_removed; relations_removed }`, `gc_item = { niceid; entity_type; title; age_days }`.
- `val init : Repository.Root.t -> t`
- `val collect : t -> max_age_seconds:int -> now:int -> (gc_item list, Item_service.error) result` — identifies eligible items without removing (dry-run). Groups by connected component via `Graph_service.connected_component`.
- `val run : t -> max_age_seconds:int -> now:int -> (gc_result, Item_service.error) result` — removes eligible items and their relations. Cascade per item: `delete_by_entity` → `delete niceid` → `delete item`.
- GC algorithm: list terminal items → filter by age → compute connected components → remove components where all items are terminal and age-eligible.
- **GC in `open_kb`**: `_run_gc root sync` reads `gc_max_age` from config. If absent, falls back to 30-day default (requirement 21). Calls `Gc_service.run`, and if anything was removed, calls `Sync_service.mark_dirty` + `Sync_service.flush` directly — not via `_with_flush`, since `t` is not yet constructed at this point in `open_kb`.
- **`bs init` change**: `cmd_init.ml` gains `--gc-max-age` optional flag (e.g., `bs init --gc-max-age 14d`). Writes the provided value or 30d default to config. Output includes `GC max age: 30d (default)` or `GC max age: 14d`. JSON output gains `gc_max_age` field.
- `Cmd_gc`: `bs gc` (run), `--dry-run` (show what would be removed), `--set-max-age 14d` (configure), `--show-max-age` (display). ~80 lines.
- Help text: EXAMPLES section covering `bs gc`, `--dry-run`, `--set-max-age`, `--show-max-age`, and `--json` example.
- Perf scenario: 500 items, 1000 relations, half terminal with old timestamps. Gate: < 50ms.

## TDD note
- Write test: terminal items older than max_age with no non-terminal neighbors are collected.
- Write test: terminal item reachable from non-terminal item is retained (transitive anchoring).
- Write test: entire component of old terminal items is removed together.
- Write test: cycle of old terminal items (A→B→C→A, all done) is collected as a single component.
- Write test: cycle containing one non-terminal item retains the entire cycle.
- Write test: `run` removes items and returns correct counts.
- Write test: `collect` returns items without removing them.
- Write test: GC hook in `open_kb` runs with default when no config key present.
- Write test: GC hook in `open_kb` uses configured value when present.
- Integration tests: `bs gc --dry-run`, `bs gc`, `bs gc --set-max-age 14d`, `bs gc --show-max-age`.
- Integration test: `bs init` outputs GC max age default.
- Integration test: `bs init --gc-max-age 7d` outputs custom GC max age.
- Integration test: auto-rebuild (delete `.kbases.db`, run `bs gc`, assert success).
- Integration test: add a GC scenario to `workflow_expect.ml` (e.g., create → resolve → wait → gc removes).
- Perf test: verify < 50ms for 500 items / 1000 relations.

title: Extract TypeidSet to Data.Uuid.Typeid
content: Define module TypeidSet = Set.Make(...) once in Data.Uuid.Typeid and remove the 3 duplicates in graph_service.ml, gc_service.ml, query_service.ml

title: Create Data.Item with shared item type and accessors
content: Create Data.Item module with item type (Todo_item | Note_item) and pure accessors: typeid, niceid, entity_type, title. Update Item_service to re-export from Data.Item. Update all service modules that define private duplicates.

title: Add map_niceid_repo_error to Item_service
content: Add map_niceid_repo_error to Item_service alongside existing map_*_repo_error functions. Update delete_service and gc_service to use it.

title: Move GC age parsing and default to Gc_service
content: Move _parse_gc_age and default_max_age_seconds to Gc_service as public functions. Update kb_service.ml and cmd_gc.ml to use them.

title: Fix timestamp of_iso8601 UTC offset calculation
content: Replace per-call UTC offset calculation in of_iso8601 with a module-level constant. Add comment explaining the approach.

title: Restructure cmd_gc.ml: extract helpers, lift context init
content: Extract _run_show_max_age, _run_set_max_age, _run_gc from cmd_gc.ml run function. Lift App_context.init to top of run before branching.

title: Persist gc_max_age during init
content: Persist gc_max_age in Lifecycle.init_kb when --gc-max-age is passed to bs init. Add integration test verifying the value is stored and readable via gc --show-max-age.

title: Remove warning 32 suppression from bin/dune
content: Remove -32 from bin/dune warning flags. The _ prefix on _blocking already suppresses warning 32 for those specific bindings.

title: Use build_specs in cmd_list relation filters
content: Replace manual relation filter construction in cmd_list.ml with a call to Service.build_specs or a shared helper, eliminating duplication with cmd_relate/cmd_unrelate.

title: Move GC config logic to service layer
content: Move GC config resolution (get_max_age, set_max_age, run with config lookup) into Gc_service or Kb_service. Keep only display formatting in cmd_gc.ml.

title: Remove root accessor from app_context
content: Remove val root from app_context.mli once cmd_gc no longer needs direct Root access.

title: Add --json examples to cmd_show and cmd_init
content: Add --json example to EXAMPLES section in cmd_show.ml and cmd_init.ml per bin principle §8.

title: Implement blocking filter in list, clean up unrelate
content: Implement --blocking as a filter in cmd_list (thread into list_spec). Remove unused _blocking from cmd_unrelate.

title: Extract Timestamp.now
content: Add Timestamp.now to Data.Timestamp, replace _now() in repository/todo.ml, repository/note.ml, and service/mutation_service.ml

title: Extract map_config_error into Item_service
content: Add map_config_error to Item_service, update gc_service.ml and lifecycle.ml to use it

title: Add timestamp accessors to Data.Item
content: Add created_at and updated_at accessors to Data.Item, remove _created_at_of_item and _updated_at_of_item from query_service.ml

title: Split show_service from query_service
content: Extract show and show_many into show_service.ml/mli. Update query_service, kb_service to wire through.

title: Extract shared cascade-delete helper
content: Extract cascade-delete helper in delete_service (delete relations, niceid, entity). Parameterize error mapping. Call from gc_service._delete_candidate.

title: Abstract Data.Timestamp.t type
content: Make Data.Timestamp.t abstract with smart constructor (validate >= 0), to_epoch, compare. Update Todo, Note, Item, repositories, JSONL, and services to use Timestamp.t instead of raw int.

title: Split query_service_expect.ml (432 lines, >300 threshold)
content: Split into query_service_list_expect.ml, query_service_count_expect.ml, query_service_sort_expect.ml per docs/test/principles.md §4

title: Split relation_service_expect.ml (363 lines, >300 threshold)
content: Split into relation_service_relate_expect.ml and relation_service_unrelate_expect.ml

title: Eliminate Unix.sleepf in unit tests via time injection
content: Inject time function into service/repo layer so unit tests can control timestamps without Unix.sleepf. Add testing principle about explicit time dependencies.

title: Restore bs list note --available validation error
content: The validation was removed but should not have been. Restore the check in query_service.ml list function.

title: Fix <missing> sentinel test in list_json_expect.ml
content: Replace <missing> sentinel assertions with explicit key-presence checks in list_json_expect.ml --available with notes test.

title: Verify dependent item state in delete --force test
content: After force-deleting kb-0, show or list kb-1 to verify what happened to the dependent item and its relation.

title: Add delete/gc/reopen/unrelate to workflow tests
content: Per docs/test-integration/architecture.md, new commands must appear in workflow tests. Add delete, gc, reopen, and unrelate to workflow_lifecycle or workflow_persistence.

title: Extract install_database helper from init_kb
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/lifecycle.ml`

## Description
Extract the inline database creation code from `init_kb` into a standalone `install_database` helper function. This is a pure refactor — `init_kb` calls the new helper instead of inline code. No behavior change.

Concretely:
- Define `let install_database ~db_file ~namespace ~gc_max_age = ...` containing the `Root.init`, `Fun.protect`, `Config.set` logic currently inline in `init_kb`.
- Replace the inline code in `init_kb` with `let* () = install_database ~db_file ~namespace ~gc_max_age in`.
- The helper is internal (not exposed in `.mli`).

## TDD note
No new tests. Run `dune runtest` and `dune build @runcheck` to confirm all 9 existing lifecycle unit tests and 11 init integration tests pass unchanged. The refactor is validated by the absence of regressions.

title: Add file_action type and uninstall_file helper
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/lifecycle.ml`
- `lib/service/lifecycle.mli`
- `test/lifecycle_expect.ml`

## Description
Add the `file_action` type (`Deleted | Not_found`) and the `uninstall_file` helper to `lifecycle.ml`/`.mli`.

`uninstall_file` is the simplest uninstall helper — it deletes a file if it exists or reports `Not_found`. Implementation:
```ocaml
let uninstall_file path =
  if Sys.file_exists path then begin Sys.remove path; Deleted end
  else Not_found
```

Export `file_action` and `uninstall_file` in `lifecycle.mli`.

## TDD note
**RED**: Write two unit tests in `lifecycle_expect.ml`:
1. `uninstall_file` on an existing file → returns `Deleted`, file is gone.
2. `uninstall_file` on a non-existent path → returns `Not_found`.

Run tests — they will fail (function does not exist yet or returns wrong output).

**GREEN**: Implement `uninstall_file` and `file_action` type. Run tests — they pass.

title: Add uninstall_agents_md helper
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/lifecycle.ml`
- `lib/service/lifecycle.mli`
- `test/lifecycle_expect.ml`

## Description
Add the `agents_md_uninstall_action` type and `uninstall_agents_md` helper.

Type:
```ocaml
type agents_md_uninstall_action =
  | File_deleted | Section_removed | Section_modified | Not_found
```

`uninstall_agents_md ~directory` implements four cases in order:
1. File does not exist → `Not_found`
2. File contents exactly equal `agents_md_template` → `Sys.remove` → `File_deleted`
3. File contains `"
" ^ agents_md_template` → remove that substring → `Section_removed`
4. File contains `agents_md_section_heading` but neither exact match → `Section_modified`
5. None of the above → `Not_found`

For case 3, use `String.sub` to extract before/after the matched substring and concatenate.

Export in `.mli`.

## TDD note
**RED**: Write tests for all four cases plus a roundtrip (install then uninstall):
1. File does not exist → `Not_found`
2. File was created by init (exact match) → `File_deleted`, file removed
3. File has other content + appended section → `Section_removed`, section stripped, rest intact
4. Section heading present but body edited → `Section_modified`, file unchanged
5. Roundtrip: `install_agents_md` then `uninstall_agents_md` on empty dir → file deleted

Run tests — they fail.

**GREEN**: Implement. Run tests — they pass.

title: Add uninstall_git_exclude helper
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/lifecycle.ml`
- `lib/service/lifecycle.mli`
- `test/lifecycle_expect.ml`

## Description
Add the `git_exclude_uninstall_action` type and `uninstall_git_exclude` helper.

Type:
```ocaml
type git_exclude_uninstall_action = Entry_removed | Entry_not_found
```

`uninstall_git_exclude ~directory` filters `.git/info/exclude` line by line:
1. File does not exist → `Entry_not_found`
2. Split on `
`, filter out lines equal to `db_filename` (i.e., `".kbases.db"`)
3. If no lines removed → `Entry_not_found`
4. Otherwise write filtered content back → `Entry_removed`

Trailing newline preservation: `String.split_on_char '
'` on a file ending with `
` produces a trailing `""`. Since `"" <> db_filename`, it survives filtering and `String.concat "
"` reproduces the trailing newline.

Export in `.mli`.

## TDD note
**RED**: Write tests:
1. Exclude file does not exist → `Entry_not_found`
2. Exclude file contains the entry among other entries → `Entry_removed`, other entries intact, trailing newline preserved
3. Exclude file exists but does not contain the entry → `Entry_not_found`
4. Roundtrip: `install_git_exclude` then `uninstall_git_exclude` → entry removed

Run tests — they fail.

**GREEN**: Implement. Run tests — they pass.

title: Add uninstall_kb orchestrator and re-export through Kb_service
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/lifecycle.ml`
- `lib/service/lifecycle.mli`
- `lib/service/kb_service.ml`
- `lib/service/kb_service.mli`
- `test/lifecycle_expect.ml`

## Description
Add the `uninstall_result` type and `uninstall_kb` orchestrator to `lifecycle.ml`/`.mli`:

```ocaml
type uninstall_result = {
  directory   : string;
  database    : file_action;
  jsonl       : file_action;
  agents_md   : agents_md_uninstall_action;
  git_exclude : git_exclude_uninstall_action;
}

val uninstall_kb : directory:string option -> (uninstall_result, error) result
```

`uninstall_kb` calls `resolve_directory` then each uninstall helper, assembling the result. It returns `Error` only if directory resolution fails.

Re-export in `kb_service.ml`/`.mli`:
- All four new types (`file_action`, `agents_md_uninstall_action`, `git_exclude_uninstall_action`, `uninstall_result`)
- `uninstall_kb` wrapper that delegates to `Lifecycle.uninstall_kb`

## TDD note
**RED**: Write orchestration-level tests:
1. Full roundtrip: `init_kb` then `uninstall_kb` on a fresh git root → all actions report deleted/removed, files are gone
2. `uninstall_kb` on a git root with no KB → all actions report not found
3. `uninstall_kb` outside a git root → returns `Error`

Run tests — they fail.

**GREEN**: Implement `uninstall_kb` and kb_service re-exports. Run tests — they pass.

title: Create cmd_uninstall CLI command and integration tests
content:
## Files
- `bin/cmd_uninstall.ml` (new)
- `bin/main.ml`
- `test-integration/uninstall_expect.ml` (new)

## Description
Create `bin/cmd_uninstall.ml` following the pattern of `cmd_init.ml`:

**Arguments:**
- `-d / --directory` — optional, git repo path (reuses the pattern from cmd_init)
- `--yes` — required confirmation flag (gate checked before calling service)
- `--json` — JSON output flag (via `Common.json_flag`)

**`--yes` gate:** If `--yes` is false, print the error message from Scenario 6 and exit 1. Do not call `Service.uninstall_kb`.

**Output formatting:**
- Text mode: print each action result as in Scenarios 1–5
- JSON mode: print structured JSON as in Scenario 8

**Output helpers:** `file_action_msg`, `agents_md_action_msg`, `git_exclude_action_msg` mapping result variants to display strings.

**main.ml:** Add `Cmd_uninstall.cmd` to the subcommand list. Add an uninstall example to root help text.

**Integration tests** in `uninstall_expect.ml` covering all 8 scenarios:
1. Clean uninstall of fully initialized KB
2. Uninstall when AGENTS.md has other content (section removed)
3. Partial state — some artifacts missing
4. Roundtrip — init then uninstall restores original state
5. AGENTS.md section edited after init (section modified warning)
6. Bare uninstall without --yes
7. Uninstall outside a git repository
8. JSON output

## TDD note
**RED**: Write integration tests first. They will fail because the `uninstall` subcommand does not exist yet.

**GREEN**: Implement `cmd_uninstall.ml`, register in `main.ml`. Run integration tests — they pass. Also run `dune runtest` to confirm no regressions.

title: Improve --yes flag doc string
content: Update --yes flag doc to explain why it is required (destructive, permanently removes KB artifacts).

title: Group related commands in help text and add principle
content: Regroup main.ml help text so related commands are adjacent. Reorder Cmd.group to match. Add bin principle 9 about command ordering.

title: Add uninstall example to root help
content: Add an uninstall example to the root help EXAMPLES section for discoverability.

title: Unit tests for Git.add_exclude and remove_exclude
content: Add tests for add_exclude and remove_exclude to test/service/git_expect.ml. Cover: file doesn't exist, entry already present/not present, entry among other entries, roundtrip add then remove, no trailing newline edge case.

title: Integration test for uninstall --json error path
content: Add an integration test in test-integration/uninstall_expect.ml that passes --json without --yes and asserts valid JSON error output with exit code 1.
